# Philosophical Questioning of Conquest Gameplay

I guess I should spare everyone the blabber (right before sharing: nah, not an option) how I am still a Deltarealm ||(obviously my expectation of 1.0, which isn't as Deltaspace as what Beau intended)|| fan and just get to the point, this is an attempt to put together attention worthy observations in a meaningful form, this is not a "jokingly complaint".

## Progression Curve

I found [what BEAUtifully sums up goal for which the levelling system update](https://discord.com/channels/749233290241769473/752599482796998677/1212755628737433600):
> the end goal being that you would always have something to do and the game isn't that easy to '100%' anymore

I've still got issue with this:
DISCLAIMER:||**THE OUTCOME OF EACH SOLUTION APPROACH IS PURELY THEORETICAL AND DOES NOT IMPLY BEING CORRECT**||!
- Item selling value decreases over time per resource type per player, ||players are encouraged to explore for other resources with full price, over time before grinding becomes too much of grid, players require consideration of buying necessary items and blocks. Has issue with transferring to this system.|| If 100%-ing meant to get efficient and high income, this solution would otherwise limit chill creative gameplay.
- ||Give players access to more items and blocks depending on the time length they have game installed. Existing player time length gets assumed in amount of owned blocks. If 100%-ing meant to have ...time unbalance? This is definitely unrelated.||
- Enchanting system (inspired by Exocraft), player gets option to upgrades their parts overtime, ||as a reward each time they complete task/get daily (or other duration) reward.|| If 100%-ing meant to acquire powerful vehicle, it would be most costly considering implementation.
- Applying not optional outcome (I think of this as the core problem), paying for lost and repaired blocks. ||It was even [brought up by Beau](https://discord.com/channels/749233290241769473/752944174663336066/1175436639363932190), although it is not that much about difficulty for advanced players as fixing the economy, it will also make for a survival game.|| If 100%-ing meant to not worry about missing anything.

***THOSE ARE NOT SUGGESTIONS, ONLY EXAMPLES***
*I hope you get it, different end game goals require different solutions, but without clearly drawn line for completing it, everyone has own preference for various goals or just making various cool stuff and fooling around for fun. For me it's also learning the game mechanics, which is also why experienced players can progress faster. With its sandbox nature players got nearly endless options for experimenting with new ideas, this gives players always something to do if they are motivated to invent, improve and use developed tech in-game.*

*There's also metric to solutions favoring specific player groups. ...*

*In the end this doesn't really matter since Beau decides for the final solution and at most these points could serve little inspiration, but it would be nice to see some experimentation on simple implementations. We as a community could optionally come up with some original ideas for solutions to these problems.*

*Me guess I are skill issue.*

# Conquering

Initially the "Conquest" mode was split into 26 by 26 square grid of sectors, I thought those could provide space for strategies over defense lines, territory expansion and military transport, now that each sector is it's own server, players pretty much just Rift in where they need. Gain is pretty much only a public spot where you can advertise your faction in-game. Claiming all station could be a goal, but no more preference base as for example multi-player connected vehicle/flagship.

In my humble opinion it would be much more interesting if players fought alongside each other for example as the four major superpowers, each having its ultimate goal conflicting each other, goal of that something is massive player cooperation just like in Foxhole https://video.fastly.steamstatic.com/store_trailers/256908034/movie480_vp9.webm?t=1664320891.

# Droneboi End Goal

I guess that would be [finally finishing the game](https://discord.com/channels/749233290241769473/839222533206704158/1138204402591731735) and droneboi paying out salary wise. There was for example the attempt to increase player attention with more frequent updates if I remember correctly, isn't marketing supposed to do that?

There is a thought of suggesting the game to streamer for reviewing, (or even two) that I use to watch, but the game needs to be worthy of even mentioning I only assumed what his measure is and whether they would be interested, It'd probably be better if you had contacted him yourself and maybe they'd like to help, or I could just randomly ask as a big fan of the game.

# Fix The Desync

When I see a long term (subjectively) first priority issue not being addressed, I will keep reminding of it||, that's usually when I still encounter networking bugs||. I assume this might not be Beau's cup of tea, or actual practical reasons ||like not stopping the development entirely||, but it won't solve on its own, it still needs to be solved somehow. ||I also wanted to include little lecture on how to solve seemingly unsolvable problems, but lol what a dumm idea.||

I'd like to help Catboi with this last one. I think of droneboi like games multiplayer to be a networking challenge, that requires extra care for performance and is not quite the same as PC equivalents Space Engineers, Avorion, Cosmoteer.
